V5-1.title: Widen the one prose measure from 52ch to 62ch (user: top section is awkwardly skinny on desktop)
V5-1.spec: Change :root --prose from 29.6rem to 35.3rem (62ch at the 19px EB Garamond body = 564.8px; chosen by rendered A/B against 52/58ch — dek settles to 3 lines, synthesis fills, ratings key fits one line). Update the derivation comment to match (62ch / 564.8px). Then sweep every REMAINING literal ch/px cap on running text and bind it to var(--prose) so the page keeps exactly ONE right edge: .movement p.lead (currently 52ch), .sc-note (currently 54ch), and any other stray text measure caps you find (grep 'ch}' and 'ch;'). Do NOT touch --measure (824px), the rail (9.5rem), or the gutter; content column is 636px which comfortably holds 565px. Confirm nothing else encodes the old 474px width (grep '474' '29.6' '52ch').

V5-2.title: Simplify the finding rail: one overall severity per bug; ENGAGES becomes dotted principle names (user: relation text wrapping in the rail looks bad)
V5-2.spec: In the .f-rail marginalia: (a) ADD an overall-severity line .f-sev directly under .f-railhead: the bug's strongest relation from findingSeverity() — text 'violates' or 'in tension', small-caps matching .fe-item voice (~.84rem), colored var(--rel-violates)/var(--rel-tension). (b) REWORK .f-engages items: each .fe-item becomes a small relation DOT + the principle NAME only — delete the ' — violates/in tension' text, .fe-relgrp and .fe-rel spans entirely (JS builder + their CSS). The dot: inline-block ~.42em circle, background var(--rel-violates|--rel-tension) per that principle's relation, margin-right ~.45em, vertically centered on the x-height, aria-hidden. Accessibility: each .fe-item carries title="<Principle> — violates|in tension" AND a .visually-hidden span ' — violates|in tension' so screen readers keep the relation. Names must no longer wrap mid-relation; long names may wrap between words (hanging indent already exists). (c) Keep .f-conf exactly as is. (d) Update the <=680px inline-rail block so the new .f-sev and dotted items flow inline cleanly (severity after the ordinal, dots inside the inline items). The per-principle relations REMAIN fully visible in the scorecard — this only de-duplicates the rail.

V5-3.title: Replace raw data IDs (F1, F6…) with display ordinals 01..N everywhere (user: 'f1/f6 don't go in order, I don't get them')
V5-3.spec: The body already sorts findings severity-first; HOIST that sort so the ordered list is computed ONCE (currently inside renderFindings) and build a map dataId -> ordinal string ('01'..'08') available to all renderers (e.g. computed in init() before rendering, passed or module-scoped). Then: (a) .f-id in the rail shows the ordinal ('01'), not the data id; anchors/hrefs KEEP the stable #f-<dataId> form. (b) Scorecard bullets: prefix each finding link with a quiet mono ordinal (.scf-ord, ~.7rem Fira Code, var(--tx-2), fixed ~1.6rem column so titles align) before .scf-title; aria-labels become 'Finding 01: <title>'. (c) Cross-references in closing sections: in the dropped/limits/credited renderers, any rendered token \bF<digits>\b whose id exists in the map is rewritten to 'finding NN' (e.g. link text 'F1' -> 'finding 01'; plain-text 'merged into F1' -> 'merged into finding 01'); unknown F-tokens are left untouched. (d) The page-nav (V5-4) uses the same ordinals. Result: a reader never sees a raw F-id.

V5-4.title: Minimal persistent side navigation (user request: always one click back to the summary; see where you are)
V5-4.spec: Build a fixed mini-nav, built by JS after render into <nav class='pagenav' aria-label='On this page'>: items top-to-bottom: '✴' -> #top (add id='top' to header.report; aria-label 'Back to top'), 'index' -> #scorecard-sec (the findings-by-principle summary), ordinals '01'..'08' -> their #f-<dataId> anchors (title + aria-label 'Finding 01: <title>'), 'coda' -> the first closing movement (give that section an id, e.g. id='coda'). STYLE: position:fixed; top:50%; translateY(-50%); left:calc(50vw - (var(--measure)/2) - 7.2rem); a single right-aligned column ~4.5rem wide; Fira Code var(--mono-micro)/.62-.68rem, letter-spacing var(--track-mono); resting color var(--tx-2) (AA-checked both themes); hover/focus var(--tx); active item var(--tx) with aria-current='true' and a small accent marker (e.g. the item's text colored var(--accent) OR a .35em accent tick — pick the quieter, test both themes). Visible ONLY @media(min-width:1080px); display:none in @media print and under 1080px; never causes horizontal scroll; z-index below the theme toggle; pointer-events only on the links. ACTIVE TRACKING: IntersectionObserver over header.report, #scorecard-sec (also covering #summary region: synthesis+scorecard both map to 'index'... NO — map #summary to the ✴/top zone is wrong; use: masthead -> '✴', synthesis+scorecard -> 'index', each .finding -> its ordinal, closings/footer -> 'coda'); exactly one item active at a time (the LAST section whose top has passed ~35% viewport); guard typeof IntersectionObserver==='undefined' -> static nav, no active state (still works as links). Clicking uses native anchor behavior (existing smooth-scroll + reduced-motion guard + scroll-margin-top apply). Keep the existing skip-link and theme toggle untouched.
